_comment: v15-Branch D: Surprise-Modulated Gradient. Hypothesis: README claims engram has 'high prediction error → up to 3× gradient' (innovation #4 — signal-modulated learning) but this mechanism was tied to MSE in v6-v11 and silently dropped in v12 when loss switched to xent. Currently every token contributes equally to the gradient — engram is, in this respect, identical to a stock transformer. Branch D restores surprise-gating in xent regime: per-sample xent scaled by surprise relative to batch mean, capped at 3x. Single-variable change in ingest.py loss path. Brain warm-started from v14-B; no freeze (Branch B already isolated vocab). v14-A was attempted but Modal GPU contention prevented completion — pivoting to D since it covers the only engram axis (#4) not yet exercised under xent.
